title: Time Management & Productivity
description: Reclaim your time, eliminate busy-work, and consistently do your most important work first.

modules.title: Understanding How You Work
modules.description: Audit your time before trying to manage it.

lessons.title: The Time Audit
lessons.content:
# The Time Audit

You cannot manage time you do not understand. A time audit reveals where your hours actually go.

## How to Run a Time Audit

1. For one week, record every activity in 30-minute blocks.
2. Use a spreadsheet, Toggl, or Clockify.
3. Categorise each block: Deep Work, Shallow Work, Meetings, Admin, Personal.
4. Calculate the percentage of time in each category.

## What You Will Likely Find
- Most people spend less than 2 hours per day on deep, high-value work.
- Meetings and interruptions consume far more time than estimated.
- Context-switching (email, Slack, browsing) is the hidden time thief.

## The Goal
Design a schedule that protects and expands your deep work blocks based on what the audit reveals.

modules.title: Prioritisation Frameworks
modules.description: Decide what to work on and what to drop.

lessons.title: The Eisenhower Matrix
lessons.content:
# The Eisenhower Matrix

Not all tasks are equal. The Eisenhower Matrix helps you decide what to do, schedule, delegate, or delete.

## The Four Quadrants

| | Urgent | Not Urgent |
|---|---|---|
| **Important** | Do now | Schedule |
| **Not Important** | Delegate | Delete |

### Quadrant 1 — Do Now
Crises, deadlines, emergencies. Minimise time here.

### Quadrant 2 — Schedule
Strategic work, learning, relationship building, planning. This is where growth happens.

### Quadrant 3 — Delegate
Interruptions, some meetings, some emails. Pass these on.

### Quadrant 4 — Delete
Time-wasting activities. Eliminate ruthlessly.

## The Goal
Most high performers spend the majority of their time in Quadrant 2.

lessons.title: Time Blocking Your Week
lessons.content:
# Time Blocking Your Week

Time blocking is the practice of scheduling specific tasks into dedicated calendar blocks.

## How to Time Block

1. **Start Sunday night.** Review the week ahead and identify your top 3 priorities.
2. **Block deep work first.** Schedule your hardest, highest-value tasks during your peak energy hours.
3. **Group shallow tasks.** Batch email, Slack, and admin into 2–3 short windows per day.
4. **Add buffer blocks.** Leave 20 % of your calendar unscheduled for the unexpected.

## Rules
- Treat calendar blocks like meetings you cannot cancel.
- Turn off notifications during deep work blocks.
- Review and adjust every Friday for the following week.

## Common Time Blocking Mistakes
- Blocking too many tasks (overestimating output).
- Not protecting blocks from meeting requests.
- Never revisiting and refining the system.
